For me, I noticed that students in line two were more active than students in line one. For today's lecture, everything was going smoothly, and there was no struggle since the lesson had been taught once. It was really good if it happened in the real class, which has a lot of students that raised hands and very good participation like this. The problem was that some students were asking a lot of questions in the class, and not all students could ask and answer the questions. Moreover, teachers should know about students and help those who struggle in class. Teachers should know about their problems, like how some students cannot see, talk clearly, and listen well. Teacher should open space for students to ask a lot of questions because this morning, I observed that students did not ask the questions.

Today's participation is really good because it is a knowledge or awaken-up for the teacher to connect with the student by understanding the method as well as the student's attitude learning and to correct them. for me, I think for disadvantages is less of the student for one teacher is fine, but if more students is needed more than one teacher because observation to student, firstly, need to catch on the attitude of them, know about how to teach them and know about the points that they could accept or need to adjust. therefore, it does not need only one teacher becuase one teacher needs to teach and need see the students,  cannot all. Moreover, not only in the class that need to help them, but also outside the class, so need a lot energy and budget too, because when we try to see a lot of studnet, we need to spend more time. For sovannarith, he's weak, inactive, like during when teacher asked in the class, she asked like a open, did not select all of them one by one, like sovannarith, in the class he did not have any activity with teacher et all, she also did not watch et all (teacher).back then, our program is good for learning of students but if less student, one teacer can see them, can adjust, but if a lot of student, cannot only one teacher, need teacher more than one.

2d:Learning of how Student Learn: Somony, He was not active, and I am not sure if he had any problems or not. I noticed that during group discussions and Q&A sessions, he seemed really eager to join his group but not really confident enough. During question and gift time, he really wanted to join with other students, but there were a lot of students raising their hands, so he lost the chance to join. Also, there were a lot of students raising their hands, and the teacher called them. I think he got a lack of encouragement from the teacher because kids really wanted the teacher to encourage them to join class activities.
I share what I saw is Sarat is silence, but she is note every sharing from teacher and his friends. I understand sarat study by noting.

What I have learned is that all students have a good connection with each other, and it is a factor that makes students able to learn from each other, like a slow learner could learn from a fast learner. For students named Ratanak and Kolboth, they were more active than the others, which means that they always asked the teacher to clarify the question or answer, which was a good way to help other students learn from them. They tried to clarify the answer that they found because they wanted to know if it was right or wrong. For Somony, she did not learn much, and she did not participate in group discussions; she looked very nervous. I have learned from her that if I were a teacher, what should I do in order to help her?

Sakada, I noticed that he was difficult to learn, but he was keeping his concentration in that class. In the class, I noticed that he looked very happy to correct the answer with the teacher, and he was keeping a very good connection with the teacher. Even though he felt stressed from the group discussion, he got very strong encouragement from the teacher. I have learned about the importance of the connection between students and learning and how it keeps students wanting to learn.

Sarim and Socheata are outstanding students, and they always raised their hands to answer the questions; it seemed like they understood everything in the lesson already. So, I had one question to reflect on: If they were understanding everything already, what have they learned today because they were always raising their hands to answer the questions? Moreover, I observed a student named Malay since the class started. She seemed isolated from the others. She seemed to not understand much in learning, and I observed that she started to learn during group discussion. She pushed herself too much to join the discussion; unfortunately, her team did not really care about her voice. To sum up, the class has two groups of students: one who is not active and another who is active. In group 2, or the active group, they were organized well, and it was easy for them to discuss and share ideas.

Sreyka was listening to the lecture, and when the teacher stopped explaining, she took that opportunity to explain to her friend that sat next to her. She was always taking note of the new learning that the teacher or her friend mentioned. The interesting point is that she was trying to be interconnected between herself and other students by moving her head to them when they answered and also nob her head to agree with their answers. Moreover, she was trying to raise her hand, but when the teacher did not call her, she felt so bad that she was not allowed to answer it. The participants in the class seemed very natural; they were not pretending.

The outstanding student, named Panha, was very good; he was taking notes, listening to the lecture silently, and trying to follow what the teacher explained. He understood a lot, and I feel like he could know the answer when the teacher was talking or asking. It reflected that if we had outstanding students, we could let them explain or present what we were learning to the other students. Or we don't need to pay much attention to him, and we can pay more attention to other students, or we can ask him to present (explain) to the class.

Today, most of the students did not take notes, but for Konica, she had all the materials for learning. For other students, they were seeing what the others did, and they followed. I think if we want them to improve, we need to encourage them and encourage them to have material either. So, during class, most of the students read the slide, and I think if we divided the group discussion to let them share ideas, they might have something to share and learn a lot.

In this situation where to make me think of my own class, what I should do to keep her from falling asleep and from taking deep breathe like that.
